name: Cocytus River
description: The Cocytus River, also known as the River of Wailing, is one of the five rivers of the Greek underworld. It is a chilling and desolate river formed from the tears of the damned, a constant reminder of their suffering and regret. Flowing through the depths of Hades, Cocytus is a place of profound sorrow and lamentation, its mournful currents echoing the eternal anguish of those trapped within its embrace. Souls who have lived lives filled with wickedness and remorse are often condemned to wander its banks, forever haunted by their past actions. The river's icy waters are said to be so cold that they can numb the senses and extinguish any hope of escape. Its presence contributes significantly to the overall atmosphere of despair and hopelessness that permeates the underworld, serving as a constant reminder of the consequences of mortal actions. Its dark waters are a physical manifestation of grief, a perpetual stream of sorrow that never ceases to flow, reflecting the eternal suffering of the damned and acting as a barrier against any hope of redemption. The Cocytus is more than just a geographical feature; it is a symbol of eternal regret and the chilling consequences of a life poorly lived, a testament to the enduring power of remorse in the afterlife.
subtitle: The River of Wailing in Hades

keyMyths.title: Passage to the Underworld
keyMyths.description: Souls must cross the Cocytus to reach the deeper realms of Hades, often requiring payment to Charon, the ferryman, if they can even reach it.

keyMyths.title: The Source of Tears
keyMyths.description: The Cocytus is said to be formed from the tears of those condemned to the underworld, making it a river of pure sorrow and regret.

sources.author: Homer
sources.description: Provides early descriptions of the underworld and its rivers.
sources.text: Provides early descriptions of the underworld and its rivers.

sources.author: Virgil
sources.description: Offers a detailed account of Aeneas's journey to the underworld and his encounters with the Cocytus.
sources.text: Offers a detailed account of Aeneas's journey to the underworld and his encounters with the Cocytus.
